content: agentColor().dot returns bg-* Tailwind classes designed for CSS circles (empty spans with rounded-full). For text characters like ● use .text instead (text-* classes). Colors.dot is for backgrounds, colors.text is for text color.

New view integration in app.js requires 3 changes: (1) import at top after existing view imports, (2) case in Router switch, (3) entry in NAV_LINKS array. Strategy view follows issues.js filter button pattern with orange active/gray inactive style.

CoordinatorChat signal integration: read agentActivityLog.value in CoordinatorChat component body (not via useEffect/useState) — it's a Preact signal so accessing .value directly in render triggers reactive updates. Transform entries with spread + id/createdAt/_isAgentActivity before merging into allMessages.

tsx subprocess ESM fix: when generating a temp .ts script that uses top-level await and spawning via tsx, name the script with .mts extension instead of .ts — tsx treats .mts as ESM unconditionally, avoiding 'Top-level await not supported with cjs output format' error that occurs when the temp dir has no package.json with type:module

Playwright smoke test pattern for SPA browser JS: consolidate all route checks into ONE test (not one per route) to avoid repeated port-4174 server startup/teardown cycles. Use page.on/page.off per-route within a single test to isolate error capture per route. Filters: isActionableConsoleError() for console events, isActionablePageError() for pageerror events — both must be filtered independently since pre-existing ReferenceErrors arrive via pageerror not console.

CostsView self-managed time filtering: CostsView fetches its own time-filtered data (filteredMetrics, modelData, dateData) when a time window is selected, falling back to initial props for All Time. This keeps the feature self-contained and avoids adding global signals to state.js. Pattern: local useState + useEffect that fires on timeWindow change.

ModelBreakdown component pattern: uses modelColor() helper that matches model name substrings (opus/sonnet/haiku) to Tailwind color classes (blue-500/green-500/yellow-500/gray-500). Total tokens = inputTokens+outputTokens+cacheReadTokens+cacheCreationTokens. Shows horizontal bars scaled to max cost + session count legend below.

DateChart component pattern: CSS-based vertical bar chart using flex layout. Max bar height constant (160px), bars scale to maxCost. formatDateShort() formats date strings as MM/DD by splitting on '-' after extracting 'YYYY-MM-DD' portion. Shows cost label above bar, date label below.

SPA view components in views/ should self-fetch their data via useEffect rather than receiving data as props from the Router component in app.js. The Router reads appState signal values and passes them as props, but Preact signals auto-tracking may not reliably trigger Router re-renders when signals update asynchronously. Self-fetching views (AutopilotView, StrategyView, CommandView mail polling) are resilient; prop-receiving views (IssuesView) are fragile.

Router key pattern for SPA view switching: in app.js Layout, the Router component must have key=${view} to force Preact to fully unmount/remount on view changes. Without this key, Preact Signals subscription tracking in the Router (which reads signal values in dashboard/costs cases but not others) can interfere with proper component lifecycle on hash navigation, causing views like StrategyView to render blank.

RawChatView in src/server/public/views/raw-chat.js: 2-panel layout (session sidebar + chat area), self-fetches data via useEffect, uses fetchJson/postJson from lib/api.js, optimistic send UI, smart scroll with isNearBottomRef. Visually distinct from agent ChatView — no agent badges, task sidebar, or thread grouping. Nav link at #chat in app.js.

E2E test files must stay synchronized with app.js routing changes (NAV_LINKS, Router cases, default route). When renaming routes (#issues to #tasks), removing routes (#command merged), or adding routes (#chat), tests must update corresponding test assertions and ROUTES arrays. Backward-compat redirects can mask test failures by passing silently.

CostsView useEffect always-fetch pattern: remove early return for null timeWindow; always fetch /api/metrics, /api/metrics/by-model, /api/metrics/by-date with or without since param. safeMetrics uses filteredMetrics as primary with initialMetrics as pre-fetch fallback. select value must use String(timeWindow) not raw null to keep Preact select controlled. Model/date chart visibility is data-driven (modelData.length > 0, dateData.length > 0) not gated on timeWindow.

Browser JS Preact select value binding for nullable state: use String(stateVar) not raw stateVar as the value prop. When stateVar is null, String(null)='null' keeps the select controlled (matches option value='null'). Raw null removes the value prop, making the select uncontrolled, causing potential issues on controlled/uncontrolled transitions.
BeadIssue normalizeIssue prefers newer bd field names: use 'raw.owner ?? raw.assignee' for assignee, 'raw.blocked_by ?? raw.blockedBy' for blockedBy — supports both old and new bd CLI output formats.

issues.js categorize(): status==='blocked' must be checked explicitly BEFORE the 'open && hasBlockers' fallback — bd can return blocked as an explicit status value, not just derived from blockedBy array.

Browser JS Preact polling pattern: extract fetch into named function, call immediately on mount, setInterval(fn, 5000) for periodic refresh, cleanup returns cancelled=true AND clearInterval(interval). Used in dashboard.js and issues.js.

Browser JS useEffect with signal dep: use [appState.someSignal.value] as dep array to trigger signal-driven effects. Reading .value during render subscribes the component; the effect fires on re-render when signal changes. Clear signal inside effect with signalRef.value = null and guard with if (!ctx) return.

Root-user guard pattern: isRunningAsRoot() defined once in src/errors.ts (returns typeof process.getuid === 'function' && process.getuid() === 0) and imported in each spawn command. Guard throws ValidationError{field:'uid'} before config loading. No-op on non-POSIX (Windows). Tests mock process.getuid = () => 0 directly (no mock.module()).

name: Task detail view pattern
description: Clickable issue cards navigate to #task/:taskId. Detail view uses 5-tab interface (Overview, Agents, Communication, Events, Merge) with async polling. Data discovery: filter agents by beadId, mail by task mention, events by agent list, merge entries by branch name. Reference InspectView for async patterns, RawChatView for layout.

name: Task detail data discovery APIs
description: APIs for task detail view: /api/issues/:id (metadata), /api/agents (list), /api/agents/:name/inspect + /api/agents/:name/events (agent-specific), /api/mail + /api/mail/thread/:threadId (communication), /api/events (timeline), /api/merge-queue (branch status). Use beadId field from sessions to correlate agents with tasks.

content: Legio uses hash-based routing: #view or #view/param. Examples: #inspect/agentName, #task/issueId, #chat (no param). App.js Router component receives {view, param} from parseHash(). Add new cases to Router switch statement. IssueCard.onClick can navigate via location.hash = `#task/${issueId}`.

SPA view components should self-fetch their data via useEffect rather than receiving props from the Router component in app.js. Preact signals auto-tracking may not reliably trigger Router re-renders when signals update asynchronously. Self-fetching views are resilient; prop-receiving views are fragile.
